statement: The integral closure of the monomial ideal M_{3,2} = (y²z², x²z², x²y²) — the Veronese cap-sum ideal of Mafi–Naderi Thm 1.6 — strictly contains M_{3,2} and gains the embedded associated prime (x,y,z) at the witness xyz, while the unmixed M_{3,2} admits no such witness (Cor 1.7; kernel-attested finite core, t = 2)
falsifiable_claim: a monomial x^(a,b,c), a,b,c < 5, in M_{3,2} but outside the cap-sum ideal; or failure of the (x,y,z) colon-witness at x^(1,1,1) for the closure; or an (a,b,c) < 5 exhibiting the same embedded-prime behaviour for M_{3,2} itself
theorem_src: theorem mafi_naderi_t2_embedded_prime : ((∀ a < 5, ∀ b < 5, ∀ c < 5, inM a b c = true → inClosure a b c = true) ∧ inClosure 1 1 2 = true ∧ inM 1 1 2 = false) ∧ (inClosure 1 1 1 = false ∧ inClosure 2 1 1 = true ∧ inClosure 1 2 1 = true ∧ inClosure 1 1 2 = true) ∧ (∀ a < 5, ∀ b < 5, ∀ c < 5, ¬ (inM a b c = false ∧ inM (a+1) b c = true ∧ inM a (b+1) c = true ∧ inM a b (c+1) = true))

references.citation: Mafi, A., & Naderi, D. (2021). Integral closure and Hilbert series of a special monomial ideal (arXiv:2112.02921). arXiv.
Huneke, C., & Swanson, I. (2006). Integral closure of ideals, rings, and modules (London Mathematical Society Lecture Note Series No. 336). Cambridge University Press.
